items.title: Colts Blue Zone Podcast 459: Owner, GM Discuss Crucial Offseason Moves
items.summary: It's been a busy offseason for the NFL and the Indianapolis Colts. This week, NFL personnel were in Phoenix for annual league meetings. In the latest Blue Zone Podcast, Mike Chappell and Matt Adams discuss comments on the offseason from owner Carlie Irsay-Gordon and GM Chris Ballard.

items.title: Two-time All-Pro, 2019 DPOY Gilmore retiring
items.summary: Two-time All-Pro cornerback Stephon Gilmore announced his retirement from the NFL.

items.title: 'Thank you football': LB Walker retires from NFL
items.summary: Linebacker Anthony Walker Jr. announced his retirement from the NFL on Thursday.

items.title: Fanatics takes over exclusive NFL card license
items.summary: A 20-year deal between Fanatics and the NFL has kicked in, consolidating the league's and players' union's trading card licenses under one company.

items.title: QB Mendoza puts on show at Indiana pro day
items.summary: Expected No. 1 pick Fernando Mendoza threw roughly 56 passes in front of scouts from all 32 NFL teams and more than 100 members of the media Wednesday.

items.title: 🐝 Buzz from the NFL league meeting
items.summary: The NFL's annual league meeting wrapped up Tuesday, and 30 of our reporters shared all the buzz from Phoenix.

items.title: Three-round NFL mock draft: Kiper, Miller, Reid and Yates' top 100 picks

items.summary: Here's what we learned during the NFL's annual league meeting in Phoenix this week.

items.summary: We're handing out report cards on big moves, including the Raiders' Kirk Cousins signing.

items.title: This year’s Colts draft just feels less exciting
items.summary: This time last year, the buzz was high. The Indianapolis Colts coveted Tyler Warren, but signs pointed to him being off the board much sooner. Things worked out, and the Colts got their man. The excitement Warren generated before and during the season was palpable. This year? This year feels totally different. As we near […]

items.title: Colts Reacts Survey
items.summary: Welcome to SB Nation Reacts, a survey of fans across the NFL. Throughout the year we ask questions of the most plugged-in Colts fans and fans across the country. Sign up here to participate in the weekly emailed surveys. Please take our survey

items.title: Colts address two positional holes in latest NFL.com mock draft—adding ‘plug-and-play’ defensive starter
items.summary: According to NFL.com’s Eric Edholm, the Indianapolis Colts will address two holes in the upcoming 2026 NFL Draft by selecting both Texas Tech linebacker Jacob Rodriguez and Clemson wide receiver Antonio Williams in Rounds 2 and 3 respectively: Round 2: No. 47 overall: Jacob Rodriguez, LB, Texas Tech Round 3: No. 78 overall: Antonio Williams, WR, Clemson Considering the […]

items.title: Solak's favorite NFL draft prospects to watch at every position: The best tape in the class
items.summary: NFL scouts are watching a lot of tape as we get ready for the 2026 draft. Which underrated players jump off the screen? Ben Solak fills out his All-Film team.

items.title: Emptying the Notebook from NFL Owners' Meetings: Anthony Richardson Sr., Justin Walley & Hunter Wohler, kicking competition
items.summary: We heard from Colts Owner & CEO Carlie Irsay-Gordon, general manager Chris Ballard and head coach Shane Steichen this week at the NFL Annual Meeting at the Arizona Biltmore in Phoenix.

items.title: NFL.com names Colts QB Daniel Jones as top ‘Next Sam Darnold’ candidate for 2026
items.summary: According to NFL.com’s Jeffri Chadiha, recently re-signed Indianapolis Colts quarterback Daniel Jones is his top candidate to become the ‘Next Sam Darnold’ for the 2026 campaign: Specifically, the 28-year-old Darnold was a former failed 2020 Top 5 pick of the New York Jets, who would bounce around to four NFL teams over the next five […]

items.title: 18-game regular season? Mixed reaction by NFL owners, coaches
items.summary: Colts, Chiefs, Browns and Patriots owners among those talking about the feasibility of expanding the schedule.

items.title: Colts GM and scouts are drafting for their jobs.
items.summary: The draft will be in Pittsburgh in less than a month and the Colts have seven picks to try and address six varying degrees of need. Andrew Aziz gave us these six needs last week and I not only agree with the positions, but the order of importance as well. A quick recap of the […]

items.title: Colts still evaluating LB options at remaining key position of need
items.summary: According to longtime general manager Chris Ballard, the Indianapolis Colts are still evaluating options at their depleted linebacker position—including both externally and internally, with a mention of even ascending 3rd-year linebacker Jaylon Carlies as a realistic replacement possibility (via Stampede Blue’s Noah Compton): After the Colts traded former All-Pro veteran linebacker Zaire Franklin to the […]

items.title: Trade market for Colts QB Anthony Richardson has reportedly been ‘soft’ so far
items.summary: According to ESPN’s Adam Schefter, the trade market for Indianapolis Colts quarterback Antony Richardson has so far been soft—meaning longtime general manager Chris Ballard hasn’t been compelled to move him quite yet: Despite reported initial interest from trade suitors such as the Cleveland Browns, Green Bay Packers, Los Angeles Rams, and Washington Commanders, Ballard himself […]

items.title: How Colts are planning to grow Josh Downs' role within offense in 2026
items.summary: Downs had 58 catches for 566 yards in 2025, but the Colts envision the fourth-year wide receiver's role as being more significant in their 2026 offense.

items.title: Colts ownership may believe in Daniel Jones, but do you?
items.summary: This isn’t a critique of Daniel Jones’ toughness. This isn’t a critique of him as a person. This is solely a critique of whether you believe Jones is the right person to lead the Indianapolis Colts at quarterback. People who know him much better than I do say yes. Is that hot air sold to […]

items.title: Still on Colts’ offseason to-do list: Determining Anthony Richardson Sr.’s future
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — There have been heavy investments in retaining the two top offseason priorities as well as addressing depth on defense. Quarterback Daniel Jones and wide receiver Alec Pierce were re-signed to contracts carrying a maximum value of a nearly a quarter-billion dollars with more than $125 million in guarantees. After pursuit of free-agent edge [...]

items.title: Colts ownership believes in re-signed QB Daniel Jones as does the current locker room

items.title: Colts GM Chris Ballard provides update on QB Anthony Richardson’s trade market
items.summary: On ‘Up and Adams’ with host Kay Adams, at the 2026 NFL Annual League Meeting, Indianapolis Colts general manager Chris Ballard indicated that while ascending 4th-year quarterback Anthony Richardson is available for trade, ‘nothing has materialized at this point’: In late February, it was reported that the Colts had granted Richardson and his representation permission […]

items.title: Colts pleased with depth, upside of defense in 2026
items.summary: The Colts turned over several key areas of their defense this offseason, with more work still yet to be done.

items.title: Colts 2026 Mock Draft Monday: College Pro Days continue to bring NFL Draft into focus
items.summary: Mock Draft Monday, our weekly roundup of mock drafts, is back amid plenty of college hosting their annual Pro Days on campuses across the country.

items.title: Carlie Irsay-Gordon: Chemistry between Shane Steichen, Daniel Jones key
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — The Indianapolis Colts’ immediate future is a matter of chemistry. Carlie Irsay-Gordon made that clear while meeting with a handful of her team’s media Sunday at the NFL owners’ meetings in Phoenix. The Colts essentially decided to head into 2026 following the same blueprint that had them among the league’s best teams after [...]

items.title: With Daniel Jones & Alec Pierce back, Colts Owner & CEO Carlie Irsay-Gordon ready to press play on re-capturing success in first half of 2025
items.summary: Colts Owner and CEO Carlie Irsay-Gordon on Sunday went into detail as to why she believes her team can hit the ground running in 2026 after a disappointing final seven games of the 2025 season.

items.title: Colts Blue Zone Podcast 458: Evaluating the Offseason So Far
items.summary: In the latest edition of the Colts Blue Zone Podcast, Dave Griffiths, Mike Chappell and Matt Adams explore the team's offseason moves and where things stack up about a month away from April's NFL Draft. Topics include: the Week 1 starter for Indianapolis, Daniel Jones' recovery, the importance of Alec Pierce and some deficiencies on [...]

items.title: How Akeem Davis-Gaither, Cam Taylor-Britt's relationships with Lou Anarumo made signing with Colts a 'no-brainer'
items.summary: Both Davis-Gaither and Taylor-Britt played for Anarumo during their time with the Cincinnati Bengals.

items.title: Nick Westbrook-Ikhine's Indiana roots give WR 'a lot to play for' with Colts
items.summary: Westbrook-Ikhine played college football at Indiana from 2015-19 and became the seventh Hoosier to reach 125 receptions and 2,000 yards in his career.

items.title: Evaluating the Colts' offseason moves
items.summary: To say the least, the Indianapolis Colts have had a busy offseason. The team re-signed Daniel Jones and Alec Pierce, both top priorities. The Colts also traded a pair of veteran players in Michael Pittman Jr. and Zaire Franklin. Other moves included signings to bolster the defensive line and secondary plus additional veteran re-signings. Colts.com [...]

items.title: Jonathan Owens' resiliency, determination leads to full-circle moment with Colts
items.summary: New Colts safety Jonathan Owens joined The Colts Show this week to discuss everything from his tryout with the Colts in 2019 to how he draws inspiration from his wife, 7-time Olympic gold medalist Simone Biles.

items.title: Colts 2026 Mock Draft Monday: One month away from NFL Draft
items.summary: Mock Draft Monday, our weekly roundup of mock drafts, is back as free agency slows down in late March.

items.title: Colts re-sign T Luke Tenuta
items.summary: Tenuta appeared in four games for the Colts in 2025.

items.title: Colts Blue Zone Podcast 457: We're Back--And the Colts Made Moves
items.summary: It's been a while, Colts fans. In a newly redecorated studio, Dave Griffiths, Mike Chappell, and Matt Adams discuss the MANY offseason moves from the Indianapolis Colts.

items.title: Colts re-sign CB Cameron Mitchell
items.summary: Mitchell appeared in eight games for the Colts in 2025, recording four passes defensed and two tackles for loss.

items.title: Colts add 2 more former Lou Anarumo players: Cam Taylor-Britt and Akeem Davis-Gaither
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – It’s never wrong to connect the dots and consider relationships when teams look to add new talent and players seek to extend their careers. That remains true with the Indianapolis Colts and defensive coordinator Lou Anarumo. In the past week, the team has added nine defensive players. Two forged a relationship with Anarumo [...]

items.title: Former Colts WR T.Y. Hilton announces retirement from NFL
items.summary: Hilton, who spent a decade in Indianapolis, ranks third in Colts history in receptions (631) and receiving yards (9,691).

items.title: Colts sign free agent WR Nick Westbrook-Ikhine
items.summary: Westbrook-Ikhine played at Indiana University and has spent six seasons in the NFL with the Tennessee Titans (2024-24) and Miami Dolphins (2025).

items.title: Colts sign free agent LB Akeem Davis-Gaither
items.summary: Davis-Gaither played for the Cincinnati Bengals under Lou Anarumo from 2020-24.

items.title: Colts free agent signings add depth and versatility to defensive line
items.summary: The Colts have signed DEs Arden Key and Micheal Clemons, and DTs Colby Wooden, Jerry Tillery and Derrick Nnadi in the last week.

items.title: Colts add veteran safety (Jonathan Owens), high-profile fan (Simone Biles)
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts’ offseason personnel push has added experience at safety as well as a high-profile fan. Consider it a package deal. Jonathan Owens has appeared in 82 games with 35 starts in seven seasons with three teams and should have the opportunity to challenge for a starting spot after the Colts saw [...]

items.title: Colts sign free agent CB Cam Taylor-Britt
items.summary: Taylor-Britt totaled 38 passes defended over four seasons with the Cincinnati Bengals.

items.title: Colts sign free agent S Jonathan Owens
items.summary: Owens has appeared in 82 games over seven seasons spent with the Houston Texans, Green Bay Packers and Chicago Bears.

items.title: Colts sign free agent DT Jerry Tillery
items.summary: Tillery was selected by the Los Angeles Chargers in the first round (No. 28 overall) of the 2019 NFL Draft.

items.title: Colts 2026 NFL free agency tracker
items.summary: We're through the first wave of NFL free agency, but there still will be plenty of moves for the Colts to make.

items.title: Colts re-sign TE Mo Alie-Cox
items.summary: Alie-Cox has appeared in 125 games over eight seasons with the Colts.

items.title: NFL offseason is young, but have the active Colts done enough?
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – We’re roughly a week into the NFL’s Rebuild-a-Roster season, and it’s worth wondering what exactly has been accomplished. Has a franchise that hasn’t reached the playoffs since Philip Rivers 1.0 in 2020 taken the necessary steps to return to relevancy? Are the Indianapolis Colts better, worse or about the same compared to where [...]

items.title: Colts sign free agent S Juanyeh Thomas
items.summary: Thomas spent his first three NFL seasons with the Dallas Cowboys.

items.title: Blake Grupe re-signs with Colts ready to take on challenge of competing for kicking role
items.summary: The Colts last week re-signed Grupe, who set a franchise record with a 60-yard field goal last season.

items.title: Colts 2026 Mock Draft Monday: After first wave of free agency
items.summary: Mock Draft Monday, our weekly roundup of mock drafts, is back after a significant week of free agent signings. How will that impact who experts predict will be available for the Colts in this year's NFL Draft?

items.title: Colts re-sign tight end Mo Alie-Cox; Alec Pierce contract details released
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — One of the longest-tenured members of the Indianapolis Colts is extending his relationship with the team. Mo-Alie-Cox, who signed with the team as an undrafted rookie in 2017, has agreed to a one-year contract, according to a source. The Schultz Report was first with the transaction. Alie-Cox, 32, is only pre-dated on the [...]

items.title: Colts re-sign WR Laquon Treadwell
items.summary: Treadwell, a 2016 first-round pick, has spent the last two seasons with the Colts

items.title: Colts re-sign tight end Drew Ogletree, add Jonathan Owens and Juanyeh Thomas
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Drew Ogletree has re-upped. The 2022 sixth-round draft pick has re-signed to help fortify the Indianapolis Colts’ tight ends room. Ogletree, 27, is heading into his fifth year and is veteran support for Tyler Warren, who enjoyed a record-breaking rookie season. He has appeared in 44 games with 20 starts and been more [...]

items.title: Colts sign free agent DT Derrick Nnadi
items.summary: Nnadi is a three-time Super Bowl champion who has played all eight of his NFL seasons with the Kansas City Chiefs.

items.title: Colts re-sign TE Drew Ogletree
items.summary: Ogletree, a 2022 sixth-round pick, has appeared in 44 games (20 starts) with the Colts in his career.

items.title: Colts hold 7 picks in NFL Draft
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts’ allotment of picks in the April 23-25 draft has been set. For now. With the trade of Michael Pittman Jr. and a seventh-round pick (No. 230) to the Pittsburgh Steelers becoming official Friday, the Colts received a sixth-round pick (No. 214). That brings their draft stash to seven picks. A [...]

items.title: Colts' full 2026 NFL Draft picks announced
items.summary: The Colts own seven picks in the 2026 NFL Draft, beginning with the No. 47 overall pick in the second round.
